text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Osei-Bonsu is a 58-year-old female with known Stage IV BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic cutaneous melanoma, currently with documented systemic progression on dabra/Mekinist targeted therapy. Restaging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis has demonstrated enlargement of the left axillary lymph node complex, interval growth of hepatic lesions with two new hepatic foci, re-enlargement of a left flank subcutaneous nodule, and new right inguinal adenopathy. LDH has risen to 285 U/L. Brain MRI requested to evaluate for intracranial metastatic spread prior to planned transition to Yervoy/nivo combination immunotherapy. Prior brain MRI performed at initial staging was negative for intracranial disease.

TECHNIQUE:
Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain was performed on a 3-Tesla system. Sequences obtained include: sagittal T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging with apparent diffusion coefficient maps, axial gradient echo (susceptibility-weighted), and axial T1-weighted images pre- and post-administration of 0.1 mmol/kg gadolinium-based contrast agent (gadobutrol). Three-dimensional post-contrast T1 volumetric acquisition with multiplanar reformations was performed for high-sensitivity detection of small parenchymal and leptomeningeal lesions. Comparison made to prior brain MRI.

FINDINGS:

Parenchyma:
The cerebral and cerebellar hemispheres are symmetric and unremarkable in signal intensity. There are no areas of abnormal T1 shortening, T2/FLAIR signal hyperintensity, or diffusion restriction to suggest infarction, hemorrhage, or infiltrative disease. No parenchymal lesions are identified. Following gadolinium administration, there is no abnormal parenchymal enhancement to suggest metastatic deposits, either at the gray-white junction or within the deep white matter. In particular, no enhancing nodules are seen in the regions typical for hematogenous metastatic seeding, including the frontal lobes, cerebellar hemispheres, or posterior fossa structures. The cortex and underlying white matter appear intact.

Leptomeninges and Dural Surfaces:
No abnormal leptomeningeal or dural enhancement is identified. The sulci, cisterns, and ventricular surfaces enhance normally and symmetrically without nodular or linear enhancement to suggest leptomeningeal carcinomatosis. The dura appears unremarkable.

Ventricular System and CSF Spaces:
The lateral, third, and fourth ventricles are normal in size and configuration. There is no hydrocephalus. The subarachnoid spaces are preserved. The fourth ventricle is in midline position without evidence of compression or obstruction.

Sellar and Parasellar Region:
The pituitary gland is normal in size, shape, and signal intensity. The pituitary stalk is midline without thickening or abnormal enhancement. No sellar or suprasellar mass is identified.

Posterior Fossa:
The cerebellum, vermis, and brainstem are unremarkable in morphology and signal. No focal cerebellar lesions are identified. The cerebellar tonsils are normally positioned well above the foramen magnum.

Calvarium and Skull Base:
The osseous calvarium and skull base appear intact and unremarkable. No focal areas of bone marrow signal abnormality to suggest osseous metastatic involvement.

Cranial Nerves:
The visualized cranial nerves, including the optic nerves, trigeminal nerves, and facial nerves, are symmetric in caliber and enhancement without evidence of perineural spread or cranial nerve involvement.

Incidental Finding:
A small T2/FLAIR hyperintense rounded lesion in the pineal region measuring approximately 4 mm is again noted, consistent with a benign pineal cyst. This is unchanged in size and appearance compared to the prior brain MRI obtained at initial staging and is without associated restricted diffusion, internal nodularity, or enhancement. This finding is of no clinical concern.

Flow Voids / Vasculature:
Major intracranial arterial flow voids are preserved bilaterally. No arteriovenous malformation or dural arteriovenous fistula is identified. The dural venous sinuses are patent on susceptibility-weighted imaging.

IMPRESSION:

1. No intracranial metastatic disease identified. No parenchymal enhancing lesions, no leptomeningeal enhancement, and no mass effect are demonstrated on this examination.

2. No evidence of hemorrhagic metastases on susceptibility-weighted sequences.

3. Stable 4 mm pineal cyst, unchanged from prior examination, benign and incidental.

4. Otherwise unremarkable brain MRI.

These findings are directly compared with the brain MRI obtained at initial staging, and no interval development of intracranial metastatic disease is identified despite progression of systemic disease on BRAF/MEK-directed targeted therapy. The brain remains clear of detectable metastatic involvement at this time.

Electronically signed by: Attending Neuroradiologist
Dictated and reviewed.
